feat(ghteardown): detect gh-issue carriers that reached done while their issue stayed OPEN (mg-6e57)

The gh-issue workflow ends by closing the GitHub issue behind a carrier work
item. That step can silently not run, and nothing notices.

mg-07ba reached `status=done, stage: merge` on 2026-07-17. The work genuinely
completed and every promise in the thread was fulfilled — but nobody closed
drellem2/pogo#89, and it sat OPEN from Jul 17 to Jul 21. No alarm fired, because
from the outside a carrier that completed its teardown and one that skipped it
are the same three characters: `done`. The miss is an ABSENCE, and an absence
emits nothing. It is invisible in exactly the direction that hurts: an issue
left open after the work lands reads to the reporter as "ignored", which is the
precise opposite of what happened.

Adds internal/ghteardown: for every carrier at `status=done`, check whether the
referenced `gh:` issue is actually closed. Surfaced as `pogo check-teardown`
(exit 1 on findings, so it can gate a schedule or CI step) and wired into
pogod's heartbeat on a coarse interval, because a detector that is never run is
the bug wearing the fix's clothes — the lesson internal/driftwatch's own doc
opens with.

REPORT-ONLY. It never closes an issue and never comments; closing an external
issue is outward-facing and stays human-gated. There is deliberately no seam in
the runner through which it could.

ISSUE STATE IS A TRI-STATE — open / closed / UNKNOWN. This is the whole
difficulty of the instrument: it asks about external state over the network, and
the reassuring answer is exactly what a broken lookup produces. A `gh` call that
fails on expired auth, rate limiting, a renamed repo, or a transferred issue
yields no "OPEN" token, so a parse that reads "not open" as closed would report
every carrier clean at precisely the moment the detector went blind. A
silent-failure detector that fails silently is worse than none, because it also
manufactures confidence. Only a positive, parsed CLOSED clears a carrier;
everything else is reported as indeterminate and counts as actionable. This is
the mg-de08 law, restated in mg-07ba's own body — "a reap must require positive
evidence of death, never absence of evidence of life" — applied to closure.

PROVEN TO FIRE, AND PROVEN ABLE TO FAIL. A detector for a silent failure that
has only ever been observed passing has not been tested, it has been assumed.
Four mutants were introduced and all four are killed by the suite: (1) treat an
open issue as clean — kills the positive control; (2) fold Unknown into clean —
kills three indeterminate tests; (3) ignore gh's exit code; (4) "not OPEN means
closed". Mutants 3 and 4 initially SURVIVED — the tri-state was documented and
implemented but never pinned — which is why issue_test.go drives the real
GHLookup through a stubbed `gh` across ten exit-code and parse cases.

Controls, verified end-to-end against real GitHub from fixtures in a scratch
store (never the live one, and no live carrier was read-modified):
  positive       done carrier + open issue          -> FIRES, exit 1
  negative       done carrier + CLOSED issue (#48)  -> silent, exit 0
  indeterminate  unresolvable repo / issue number   -> reported, NOT "closed"
  declared-open  carrier with a `gh-open:` line     -> listed, not a miss

THE PREDICATE AND THE "OPEN ON PURPOSE" QUESTION. The predicate is
`workflow: gh-issue` + `status=done` + an open `gh:` issue. It is deliberately
NOT gated on `stage: merge`: the mayor's own carrier note records mg-c155's
stage field as "misleading", so stage is not load-bearing evidence.

That predicate fires on carriers whose issue is open legitimately — mg-c155 is
done while drellem2/pogo#88 correctly waits on the reporter for a patch Daniel
asked for, and closing it would retract an outstanding request. A detector that
shouts about #88 every run gets muted long before the run that matters. The
resolution is an explicit, machine-readable `gh-open: <reason>` line in the
carrier body: such a carrier is not a miss and never mails, but is still LISTED
under "declared open", because a suppression that outlives its reason is the
same silent absence this package exists to catch. Nothing infers the line — a
human writes it — so an un-annotated carrier always fails toward being noticed.

Tests never touch the live store: MGSource.resolveRoot returns a per-binary temp
dir under `testing.Testing()` and NEVER `~/.macguffin`. This is a test-safe
DEFAULT rather than an opt-in helper, which is the whole lesson of mg-da48 —
witness_test.go shipped a `sandboxWitness(t)` helper and the only tests that
remembered to call it were the ones whose subject WAS the witness; the rest wrote
phantom polecats into the live store. An opt-in guard is only ever remembered by
the tests that least need it. TestCarriersNeverTouchTheLiveStore asserts every
`mg` invocation carries --root.

Coverage boundary, stated rather than hidden: archived carriers are scanned only
under --archived, since each carrier costs a network round-trip and the store
holds ~80 archived against a handful of done. A carrier archived while its issue
is open is the most forgotten case of all, and the default scan will not see it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
